tactical_shape: High, wide attacking play; early pressure down the flanks
Craft-laden midfield of Bruno Guimarães, Lucas Paquetá and Casemiro
Matheus Cunha used as the central attacker
Vinícius Júnior and Raphinha operating as wide forwards
standout_players: Vinícius Júnior — sharp, buzzy and hungry; drove the wide threat
Raphinha — high-grade wide play before going off injured; dangerous in space
Matheus Cunha — slick and inventive in the centre; scored a quickfire double
Bruno Guimarães — part of a really good midfield
Lucas Paquetá — part of a really good midfield
Casemiro — part of a really good midfield
Carlo Ancelotti — kept Brazil calm and flexible, noncommittal on a fixed identity
fatigue_injuries: Raphinha suffered a hamstring injury, described as a genuine structural threat to Brazil's progress
home_read.momentum: Brazil look dangerous and full of elite talent, but still a strange, unsettled side whose ceiling is unclear.

tactical_shape: Defended cautiously but left huge open spaces in the wrong places
Occasionally advanced up the pitch, exposing the flanks
standout_players: Johny Placide — beaten for the goals after making the initial block on Vinícius's shot
Hannes Delcroix — involved in the deflection that led to Cunha's first goal
Pierrot — shifted weight the wrong way on Cunha's second
away_read.momentum: Bottom of Group C with no goals and no points; their tournament is effectively over, and they now move to a final fixture against Morocco.
